policy: A screenshot or memory-mode run is not enough for benchmark UI proof.
Every benchmark UI run must start from a fresh live room and use the public @nodeagent lane.
Every expected deliverable type must be exported/downloaded from the browser, reopened from disk, and passed to the benchmark scorer or verifier.
SpreadsheetBench requires workbook export/reopen/scoring; BankerToolBench requires Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and PDF package handling.
Runner-only evidence is useful plumbing, but it does not satisfy live-browser fresh-room coverage.

deliverableTypes.label: Excel workbook
validation: download candidate workbook from the room
run workbook scorer, formula recompute, and format diff where applicable

deliverableTypes.label: PowerPoint deck
validation: download candidate deck from the room
hand candidate deck to the BankerToolBench verifier

deliverableTypes.label: Word document
validation: download candidate memo from the room
hand candidate memo to the BankerToolBench verifier

deliverableTypes.label: PDF
validation: download candidate PDF from the room
hand candidate PDF to the BankerToolBench verifier

deliverableTypes.label: CSV/table export
validation: download candidate CSV when a task requests table export

deliverableTypes.label: Image/asset export
validation: download or inspect image assets when a task produces them
include assets in verifier package manifests

gates.label: Create or join a fresh live room through the browser UI
Upload official benchmark input files through the UI
Send the official instruction through public @nodeagent chat
Show visible agent progress or streamed text while work runs
Export or download every expected deliverable type from the UI
Reopen downloaded artifacts from disk before scoring
Hand artifacts to the official or benchmark-faithful scorer
Persist trace, screenshot, and video evidence for each run
Do not use memory-mode demo seeds for benchmark claims
